Fleet vs Megatron ref — best/worst/spread%

fleetnmeanstd bestworstspread% mean vs Megatron Δσ-significance
σ-significance = Δ / sqrt(SEM_nano² + SEM_meg²). |σ| < 1: indistinguishable; |σ| > 2: statistically separated.

Notes

Setup: 10-seed × 3 fleet (Adam MoE / Muon MoE / Dense Adam), all init_from='scratch', on PAI quota1shcr2h7uae + quotadbz1mvpy1v5. Megatron refs are real scaling_moe_00196_noise{1-5}_v2 (Adam) + scaling_moe_00196_muon_noise{1-5} (Muon) + scaling_dense_00107_noise{2-5}_full (Dense).

Muon LR1× fix: discovered nano default muon_lr = lr × 33 (modded-nanogpt convention) was 33× too aggressive. Changed to lr × 1 (Megatron muon_lr_multiplier=1.0). Pre-fix s1337 landed at tail-100 = 2.9913 (red-dash trace in muon panel); post-fix lands at 2.7889 ± 0.0039.

Dense Adam beats ref: nano 3.1049 vs Megatron 3.1131 — 2.4σ statistically lower. Likely because nano's data path / DDP setup is slightly more efficient at this small scale.

Muon < Adam in seed-spread: Muon 0.40% vs Adam ~0.97% spread/mean — NS5 + spectral-normalize regularizes inter-seed variance.
